name: BILE DUCTS
definition: intrahepatic biliary system runs parallel to portal venous supply of liver SUBVESICAL / ACCESSORY BILE DUCTS small ducts from liver bed draining directly into GB TRIANGLE OF CALOT anatomic space bordered by CHD medially, cystic duct inferiorly, and cystic artery

demographics.Genetics: PKHD1 (polycystic kidney hepatic disease 1) gene codes for
demographics.Pathology: mutation of PKHD1 gene → abnormal cilia → abnormal ductal plate formation

dominant_finding: distal cystic duct posterior to CBD (in 95%), anterior to CBD (in 5%)
distribution: distal cystic duct posterior to CBD (in 95%), anterior to CBD (in 5%)
discriminating_features: distal cystic duct posterior to CBD (in 95%), anterior to CBD (in 5%)
cystic duct parallels extrahepatic bile duct (implies common fibrous sheath)

Cystic duct: distal cystic duct posterior to CBD (in 95%), anterior to CBD (in 5%)
cystic duct parallels extrahepatic bile duct (implies common fibrous sheath)
gallbladder drains directly into common bile duct

important for donor of right hepatic lobe transplant
